channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump says Newsom is to 'blame' for 'apocalyptic' wildfires
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump slammed California Gov. Gavin Newsom for the wildfires raging in California, pinning blame for the fire on his environmental policies.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Dems accused of 'stonewalling' Tulsi Gabbard confirmation after GOP demands quick hearing
item.description: Tulsi Gabbard and the Trump transition team have accused Senate Intelligence Committee Vice Chairman Mark Warner of "stonewalling" her confirmation process.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Germany's leader says all countries must respect existing borders, in a rebuttal of Trump
item.description: German Chancellor Olaf Scholz says the principle of inviolability of borders applies to every country, however powerful.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Justice Department says it plans to release only part of special counsel's Trump report for now
item.description: The Justice Department said Wednesday that it will release special counsel Jack Smith’s findings on Donald Trump’s efforts to undo the results of the 2020 presidential election but will keep under wraps for now the rest of the record focused on the president-elect's hoarding of classified documents
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Drug dealers could be charged with murder under new Virginia fentanyl plan
item.description: Virginia Republicans, facing a one-seat minority, hope to tackle the fentanyl crisis with stringent punishments including murder charges for some drug dealers, lawmakers say.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Carter and Biden's long friendship had wrinkles. It will be on display a final time with a eulogy
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Joe Biden is the consummate Washington insider. Jimmy Carter was anything but. Yet the 46th and 39th U.S.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW and DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump files emergency petition to Supreme Court to prevent sentencing in NY v. Trump
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump on Wednesday morning filed an emergency petition to the United States Supreme Court in an effort to block his sentencing in New York v. Trump.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Former FBI informant will be sentenced for bogus bribery claim about President Biden and son Hunter
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Prosecutors will ask a judge on Wednesday for six years in prison for a former FBI informant whose bogus story about President Joe Biden and his son Hunter accepting bribes became central to Republicans' impeachment effort.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Kansas secretary of state launches 2026 GOP gubernatorial bid for seat held by 2-term Democrat
item.description: Secretary of State Scott Schwab is running for governor of Kansas in 2026, launching his campaign exclusively with Fox News Digital.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Colorado governor walks a tightrope in approaching Trump. Deportations likely to be the first test
item.description: DENVER (AP) — Colorado Gov. Jared Polis donned safety glasses and seized the handle of the electric saw, guiding the buzzing blade down through a stack of printed-out executive orders dating back decades.
item.dc:creator: JESSE BEDAYN and NICHOLAS RICCARDI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden admin appeals plea agreements with Guantánamo detainees, including 9/11 mastermind
item.description: The Biden administration is asking for a federal appeals court to temporarily block a plea deal agreement with three detainees in Guantanamo Bay, Cuba.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Future of Florida's shores: Biden's offshore oil drilling ban faces political backlash
item.description: President Joe Biden issued a ban on offshore oil drilling in federal waters, including the Gulf of Mexico. But President-elect Donald Trump plans to reverse the move when he takes office in less than two weeks.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 2 death row inmates reject Biden's commutation of sentences
item.description: Shannon Agofsky and Len Davis believe the life sentences put them at a legal disadvantage with them planning to seek appeals based on claims of innocence.
item.dc:creator: Kyler Swaim, NewsNation

item.title: Judge temporarily blocks release of special counsel report on Trump cases as court fight simmers
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge on Tuesday temporarily blocked the public release of special counsel Jack Smith's report on investigations into Donald Trump as an appeals court weighs a challenge to the disclosure of a much-anticipated document just days before the president-elect reclaims office.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NY governor proposes $110 million for new child care centers
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- Continuing a theme of proposing splashy and spendy new proposals aimed at keeping families in New York, Gov. Kathy Hochul took aim at the lack of child care centers Tuesday. Hochul wants to create a $110 million fund to support the construction of new child care facilities.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: Board of Supervisors splits votes on new chair following Vargas' exit
item.description: The San Diego County Board of Supervisors punted a vote on who will succeed Nora Vargas as chair after competing motions to elevate either Terra Lawson-Remer or Jim Desmond to the role failed in votes down partisan lines.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, Fox 5

item.title: Curator talks archiving Walz’s bid for VP, preserving political legacies
item.description: While brief, Gov. Tim Walz’s bid for vice president will add another chapter to his legacy in politics, and the Minnesota Historical Society has gotten a jumpstart on preserving it.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer and Gracie Stockton, MPR News

item.title: Trump's praise of Carter in death after jeering him in life deepens a contradictory relationship
item.description: PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — Living to 100 let Jimmy Carter fulfill his wish to vote for Democratic Vice President Kamala Harris against Republican Donald Trump in November. His death means flags at the White House will be at half-staff when Trump regains the presidency on Jan. 20.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT and MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump refuses to rule out use of military force to take control of Greenland, Panama Canal
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump on Tuesday said he would not rule out the use of military force to seize control of the Panama Canal and Greenland, as he declared U.S. control of both to be vital to American national security.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT and ZEKE MILLER Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: New labels will help people pick devices less at risk of hacking
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The federal government is rolling out a consumer labeling system designed to help Americans pick smart devices that are less vulnerable to hacking. Under the voluntary program, called the U.S.
item.dc:creator: DAVID KLEPPER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Canadian PM Justin Trudeau resigns
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau announced his resignation Monday in the face of rising discontent over his leadership, and after the abrupt departure of his finance minister signaled growing turmoil within his government.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Canada’s Trudeau resigns as Liberal Party leader, spelling end to time in power
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau announced his resignation in the face of rising discontent over his leadership, and after the abrupt departure of his finance minister signaled growing turmoil within his government.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Biden blocks some new offshore drilling ahead of Trump takeover
item.description: President Biden has blocked new offshore drilling for oil and gas in several parts of the country about two weeks before President-elect Trump takes office. Biden has announced that he’ll block new drilling off the entire East Coast, as well as California, Oregon and Washington state.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, Fox 5

item.title: Harris readies to certify Trump’s election win – and her decisive defeat
item.description: Kamala Harris is readying on Monday to certify the 2024 election results, affirming President-elect Trump’s victory as well as her decisive defeat to him after a whirlwind run for commander in chief.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Higher Social Security payments coming for millions of people from bill that Biden signed
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden on Sunday signed into law a measure that boosts Social Security payments for current and former public employees, affecting nearly 3 million people who receive pensions from their time as teachers, firefighters, police officers and in other public service jobs.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Melania Trump to debut documentary film on Amazon in 2025
item.description: “As a private person, who has often been the subject of public scrutiny and misrepresentation, I feel a responsibility to clarify the facts,” First Lady Melania Trump said.
item.dc:creator: Tanya Noury, NewsNation

item.title: Judge denies Jan. 6 rioter’s bid to attend Trump inauguration
item.description: Judge Royce Lamberth denied the request in a Friday court order, reflecting on the event that took place after the 2020 presidential election.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: Fight over Florida tuition for undocumented immigrants continues years later
item.description: A Florida senator wants to repeal a law that grants in-state tuition rates to undocumented immigrants. State Senator Randy Fine says he just wants to put Floridians first, but the bill is facing backlash from across the aisle.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Lombardo's State of the State speech set for Jan. 15
item.description: Gov. Joe Lombardo will give his 2025 State of the State Address on Wednesday, Jan. 15, at 6 p.m., according to a Friday statement from the governor's office.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Biden to award Medal of Honor to Korean and Vietnam War veterans
item.description: The Medal of Honor, the United States’ highest military decoration, is awarded to service members who display exceptional valor in combat.
item.dc:creator: Lexie Schapitl, MPR News

item.title: New laws in NJ impact minimum wage, data privacy, and reading content
item.description: TRENTON, N.J. (PIX11) -- With every new year come new laws -- each impacting someone somewhere in the Garden State. New Jersey's minimum wage is increasing to $15.49 an hour for most workers.
item.dc:creator: Jim Vasil, PIX 11
